perturbation.original_text: 8. NON-PUBLIC INFORMATION. MCI has outstanding publicly-held securities and the Confidential Information contains material non-public information. The Company acknowledges that it is (i) aware, and has advised or will advise its Representatives, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities and (ii) familiar with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, and the Company agrees that it will neither use nor permit any of its Representatives to use any Confidential Information in violation of such Act or rules or regulations, including without limitation Rule 10b-5.
perturbation.changed_text:
16. MODIFICATIONS. No provision of this Agreement may be waived, amended or modified except by the written agreement of the Company and MCI.

8. NON-PUBLIC INFORMATION. MCI has outstanding publicly-held securities and the Confidential Information contains material non-public information. The Company acknowledges that it is (i) aware, and has advised or will advise its Representatives, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person in possession of material non-public information about a company from purchasing or selling securities of such company, and from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person may purchase or sell such securities and (ii) familiar with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, and the Company agrees that it will neither use nor permit any of its Representatives to use any Confidential Information in violation of such Act or rules or regulations, including without limitation Rule 10b-5.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the clause regarding non-public information and insider trading to the 'Modifications' section makes it less prominent and less likely to be noticed, potentially undermining its enforceability. This is because readers might not expect a crucial legal warning about securities laws to be buried within a clause about how the agreement can be changed.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 10b-5 prohibits deceptive practices, including insider trading. By burying the acknowledgement of insider trading prohibitions within a section on modifications, the company reduces the likelihood that employees and representatives will be adequately informed of their legal obligations under the Securities Exchange Act, potentially leading to unintentional violations of the rule.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The structural change places a critical disclosure related to insider trading (Rule 10b-5) within a section concerning contract modifications. This undermines its prominence and accessibility. Snippet 1 states that it is unlawful to employ any device to defraud in connection with the purchase or sale of any security. By burying the insider trading clause, the altered text reduces the likelihood that it will be noticed and followed, potentially leading to unintentional violations of Rule 10b-5. Therefore, the modification creates a structural flaw that contradicts the intent and practical application of the law.

perturbation.original_text: 12. REMEDIES. It is understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Company and that MCI shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunction, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by the Company but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to MCI, including remedies pursuant to applicable laws relating to trade secrets.
perturbation.changed_text:
5. NO REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES. Neither MCI nor any of its respective officers, directors, employees, representatives or agents makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy and completeness of any Confidential Information provided by it, and no liability shall result to MCI from its use, except as set forth in a definitive agreement for a Transaction. Only the representations and warranties that are made in a definitive agreement for a Transaction, when, as, and if it is executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, shall have any legal effect.

12. REMEDIES. It is understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by the Company and that MCI shall be entitled to equitable relief, including specific performance and injunction, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by the Company but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to MCI, including remedies pursuant to applicable laws relating to trade secrets.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the clause about remedies to be within 'No Representations or Warranties' will make people less likely to enforce it. This is because the clause about remedies has to be easily enforced, but hiding it under the clause about no representations or warranties could mislead someone reading the contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: General contract law provides for various remedies in case of breach, including equitable remedies like specific performance. By relocating the remedies clause to a section where it is less visible, the agreement potentially impedes the parties' understanding of their rights and remedies in case of a breach, undermining the intent of contract law.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that moving the remedies clause makes it less likely to be enforced, and the law explanation states that it undermines the intent of contract law. The structural flaw, as defined, occurs when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. Because the remedies clause was moved to a new section regarding 'No Representations or Warranties', this makes it harder to find and enforce.
